user_text: Hey, it's not Jagan. I told you to rename my name.
note: Session 86 live run (2026-04-22): LLM hallucinated {'name':'Kara'} from history; classifier correctly abstained (value=None, conf=0.80, intent=assign_own_name). _intent_allows skipped grounding because extracted_value was None — hallucinated 'Kara' rename slipped through. Ground truth: casual_conversation (user complaining about misname, not assigning). Post-grounding-gap fix (Session 87): this row MUST reject update_person_name({'name':'Kara'}) because 'kara' is not in user_text.

Session 86 live run: classifier labeled live_data_query at 0.95 — imperative phrasing misled it into treating a state-recall question as external live-data lookup. Ground truth: casual_conversation (asking what the system already knows about the user). Regression row documents the mislabel for future calibration passes.

user_text: No, I never said Kara, I said call me Gladiator.
note: Session 86 live run: multi-part utterance (denial of prior attempted rename + new assignment). Classifier correctly labeled assign_own_name value='Gladiator' conf=0.95. Positive regression guard — confirms multi-part phrasing handling stays correct through future prompt changes.

user_text: Hi Kara, I'm Sarah, Jagan's classmate
note: Session 94 live-canary fix: classifier misread 'Hi Cara, Imlexi, Jagan's classmate' as assign_system_name value='Cara'. Root cause: no counter-example in the prompt for the 'Hi X, I'm Y' pattern where X is the greeting target (system's existing name) and Y is the speaker's self-intro. Expected: classifier correctly extracts the SECOND name (Y) as assign_own_name, not the first (X) as assign_system_name. This row locks in the fix.

Phase 3B.2: vocative = system name Kara, asking the AI. NOT direct_address_to_person — classifier must route to live_data_query (the underlying intent). Tests that AI-directed vocative doesn't accidentally trigger the user-to-user skip path.

Phase 3B.2: vocative = Jagan (NOT the system). User is asking another person in the room, not the AI. Classifier must emit direct_address_to_person with extracted_value='Jagan' so pipeline can skip the LLM response.

user_text: Lexi said the movie was good
note: Phase 3B.2: Lexi is the SUBJECT of the sentence, not the vocative. User is reporting what Lexi said, not addressing Lexi. Must NOT be classified direct_address_to_person.

user_text: Kara, ask Jagan about the weather
note: Phase 3B.2: compound address — vocative is Kara (system name), Jagan is the object of the ask. Classifier routes to live_data_query because Kara is being addressed; Jagan being mentioned is incidental.

user_text: Hey Lexi, are you feeling better?
note: Phase 3B.2: interjection + vocative name at start = clear user-to-user address. Classifier must emit direct_address_to_person with value='Lexi'.

user_text: Maybe an EV. Range anxiety is a thing though, right?
note: Spec-1 follow-up (2026-04-28): relabeled from removed `topical_participant_response`. AI was active in the EV topic + 'right?' tag invites response — under the surviving label space this is conversational engagement with the AI.

user_text: So we should pick something for movie night?
note: Spec-1 follow-up (2026-04-28): relabeled from removed `topical_participant_response`. Group-directed question after AI participation — falls through to casual_conversation in the new label space.
Bystander question — AI hasn't been part of the Sarah thread; conversation is between two other people. Should classify as casual_conversation, not direct_address_to_person.
Substantive close, no question, no group invite — bare statement doesn't invite response. Must classify as casual_conversation.

user_text: Hey Lexi, can you pass the salt?
note: Friends-style 'Hey {Name}' must classify as direct_address_to_person — explicit-addressing regression guard.

user_text: No Kara, I was talking to Lexi.
note: Spec-1 follow-up Item 7: linchpin example — user corrects the AI's previous turn. Pipeline applies outcome supervision to scenarios that voted for the wrong label on turn N-1. extracted_value names the intended target.

user_text: Stop Kara, that wasn't for you.
note: Spec-1 follow-up Item 7: correction without explicit target — pipeline still decrements wrong scenarios; correct target left unspecified, graph re-balances organically.
Spec-1 follow-up Item 7 negative discriminator: utterance LOOKS like a correction but is disagreement directed AT the AI. Must NOT trigger correction_to_previous_response.

user_text: who said I'm in office I don't have any job and I don't go to office
note: P0.S10 canary 2026-05-27: brain misrouted topic-denial (work + office facts) to report_identity_mismatch, triggering dispute. Classifier must route to personal_statement (denies activity/topic, not identity). D1 ASSERTION-DOMAIN RULE + D2 tool description + D3 IDENTITY_DENIAL_PATTERNS gate together prevent recurrence.

note: SB.6: held-object camera query. Routes to the object-detection visual channel.
SB.6: object-in-view camera query. Requires looking at the live frame.

note: SB.6: scene-description camera query (MORE_DETAILED_CAPTION).
SB.6: scene-description camera query.
SB.6: OCR camera query (OCR). Text in front of the camera, not a web lookup.
SB.6: OCR camera query.
SB.6 contrastive lookalike: shares the 'what' shape with visual_query but asks for WORLD info (web lookup), NOT the camera. The collision guard — must stay live_data_query.

user_text: What's the score of the game?
note: SB.6 contrastive lookalike: world info, NOT the camera. A blind person could answer it from a web search.

user_text: What's the price of bitcoin right now?
note: SB.6 contrastive lookalike: live-data world info, NOT a visual_query despite the 'right now' immediacy.

user_text: Do you see why the sky is blue?
note: SB.6 negative trap: contains 'see' but is a world-knowledge question, NOT a camera-look. Must NOT route to visual_query.
